artifacts.content:
Hi Tía Jasmine,

Figured I’d actually write instead of just sending memes. Things are a little calmer here than last summer when Dad’s HVAC hours got cut and we were like two months behind on rent at the 32nd Street apartment. We were short about $2,100 for July–August 2023, so I picked up 20–24 hours a week at Desert Peaks to help with groceries and SRP. It was rough, but we’re current on rent now and Dad’s getting more calls again.

I’m trying to balance work better this year. Carlos had me on a bunch of 3:30–11:00 pm closing shifts in April–May 2024, and between those, practice, and homework I dropped 14 pounds without really noticing. I actually almost passed out at work and ended up at Phoenix Baptist Hospital on April 19 with this near-syncope thing. They said it was just the combo of not eating enough and being wiped out, but it scared Mom.

On top of that, I’ve been doing weekly Zoom sessions with a therapist, Dr. Vanessa Ortiz at Valley Hope Behavioral Health, since March after I had a panic episode during a club tournament in Tucson. It was weird—heart racing, couldn’t catch my breath before a game. She’s helping me with pre-game anxiety and sleep stuff. It’s not like I’m falling apart, just trying not to let everything pile up like last year.

Anyway, ASU in 2025 still looks real. I’m leaning hard toward Exercise Science so maybe one day I can help kids who push themselves too much like I do. How are you and the design projects? Tell Abuela in Tucson I said hi.

Love,
Ethan

Personal check-in – don’t repeat old patterns

1) Food / training history
- Remember March–April 2020: running alone at Steele Indian School Park every day, barely eating, dropped like 18 pounds in six weeks. Dr. Karen Lopez at Phoenix Children’s Pediatrics on Thomas Rd had me coming in every 2–3 weeks April–June 2020 to make sure I wasn’t disappearing. I’ve done way better the past 3+ years—don’t go back there.
- Then junior year: the whole disordered eating thing again. From August 2022 to March 2023 I was in the Phoenix Children’s Adolescent Eating Disorder Program, working with dietitian Karen Alvarez. Lost about 12 pounds and my testosterone tanked. The structured meal plan she set up actually works when I follow it.

2) Work / overdoing it
- This spring I let Carlos schedule me on those 3:30–11:00 pm closes at Desert Peaks during school. Combined with practice and skipping meals, I ended up at Phoenix Baptist Hospital on 4/19/24 for near-syncope and another 14-pound drop. No more closing shifts on school nights.

3) Mental health
- Fall 2022 after Javier’s crash on I-10 near Tolleson (10/14/22) was awful, but talking to Dr. Angela Perez at Desert Vista for six weekly sessions late Oct–early Dec helped. Same idea now with Dr. Vanessa Ortiz at Valley Hope—stick with the weekly telehealth sessions through season.

Bottom line: eat regularly, sleep, say no to extra shifts, and ask for help before things get bad.

Hi Mom,

Just wanted to put everything in one place before school gets crazy again.

Therapy: I’m going to keep the weekly telehealth with Dr. Vanessa Ortiz at Valley Hope Behavioral Health. Ever since that panic episode at the club tournament in Tucson back in March, the Thursday night Zoom calls have helped a lot with pre-game nerves and sleep. It feels kind of like when I saw Dr. Angela Perez at Desert Vista after Javier’s accident on I-10 near Tolleson on 10/14/2022—those six weekly sessions from late October to early December 2022 made a big difference, and this feels similar.

Work: I emailed Carlos already, but can you back me up if he calls? I really can’t do the 3:30–11:00 pm closing shifts on school nights anymore, especially during soccer and track. The doctors at Phoenix Baptist Hospital said the near-syncope on April 19, 2024 was basically from the 14-pound weight loss, stress, and not eating enough during those shifts.

Food: I know you and Dad worry about me slipping back into the old stuff. I’m still using the meal plan Karen Alvarez from the Phoenix Children’s Adolescent Eating Disorder Program set up when I was there August 2022–March 2023. It’s keeping my weight and energy steady.

I promise I’ll tell you if I start feeling off again—physically or mentally. Thanks for driving me to all these appointments and not making me feel weird about it.

Love,
Ethan

metadata.subject: Plan for this season (therapy, work, food)

artifacts.content:
Event: Telehealth counseling with Dr. Vanessa Ortiz

Time: Thursdays, 7:00–7:45 pm (recurring)
Location: Bedroom desk, headphones on, door closed
Attendees: Ethan Ramirez, Dr. Vanessa Ortiz (Valley Hope Behavioral Health Clinic, Phoenix)

Notes:
- Started these weekly telehealth sessions in March 2024 after that panic episode during the club soccer tournament in Tucson (heart racing, felt like I couldn’t breathe during warm-ups). Goal is to manage performance anxiety and sleep, especially once the 2024–25 school year sports season gets going.
- Things to remember to bring up:
  • Nights I end up at Abuela Elena’s place in Mesa when Mom and Dad are arguing. In November–December 2023 it was like 4–5 nights where I slept on her couch after late-night fights at the apartment on 32nd Street. Still happens sometimes, just not as often.
  • Stress about money since Dad’s hours got cut last July and we fell two months behind on rent (about $2,100). Even though we’re caught up now, I still feel like I have to say yes to every shift at Desert Peaks.
  • Any signs I’m slipping back into old eating/exercise habits from 2020 or junior year.
- Try to schedule around soccer and track so I’m not doing Zoom therapy totally wiped out after practice.

Medical / performance history – for myself

Heart stuff:
- January 2023: abnormal EKG at Banner – University Medical Center Phoenix because of family history (Uncle Javier Collins died suddenly at 39). Started yearly follow-ups with cardiologist Dr. Priya Desai. I’m cleared for full participation, but I need to keep those appointments.

Injuries:
- Old right ankle sprain from soccer at 16, plus the work injury on 2/3/24. Sprained the same right ankle moving a 60-pound box in the Desert Peaks stockroom. Workers’ comp sent me to Phoenix Sports Rehab, where Mark Liu fitted a custom right ankle brace in February 2024. I’m supposed to wear it for games and long stockroom shifts.

Eating / weight:
- March–April 2020: restrictive eating and excessive running at Steele Indian School Park, lost ~18 pounds in six weeks. Pediatrician Dr. Karen Lopez at Phoenix Children’s Pediatrics on Thomas Rd monitored me every 2–3 weeks April–June 2020.
- August 2022–March 2023: Phoenix Children’s Adolescent Eating Disorder Program with dietitian Karen Alvarez after dropping 12 pounds and having low testosterone junior season. Still following her structured nutrition plan.
- April 19, 2024: near-syncope at Phoenix Baptist Hospital after 14-pound unintentional weight loss from stacked practices and 3:30–11:00 pm closing shifts.

Mental health:
- Fall 2022: six weekly counseling sessions with school psychologist Dr. Angela Perez at Desert Vista after teammate Javier Morales died in a single-vehicle accident on I-10 near Tolleson (10/14/22).
- March 2024–present: weekly telehealth with Dr. Vanessa Ortiz at Valley Hope for performance anxiety and sleep.

Use this to remember what NOT to repeat.

[Group chat: “DVHS Squad”]

Ethan: Yo, if I look dead in AP Gov tomorrow, it’s not just senioritis lol.

Marco: What happened now?

Ethan: Parents were at it again. I ended up driving to Abuela Elena’s apartment in Mesa around 11:30. Crashed on her couch like I did back in Nov–Dec when they were going at it all the time. I swear it was like 4–5 nights in a row last fall.

Luis: Damn man, you good?

Ethan: Yeah, just tired. At least it’s not like Fall 2022 when Javier’s accident on I-10 near Tolleson happened. Remember 10/14/22? That whole month was a blur. I was meeting with Dr. Perez in the Desert Vista counseling office once a week for like 6 weeks just to keep it together.

Marco: You still talking to someone?

Ethan: Yeah, I do Zoom with this therapist, Dr. Ortiz at Valley Hope, every Thursday night since I had that panic thing at the Tucson tournament in March. Helps a bit.

Luis: Respect for actually getting help, bro.

Ethan: Tbh I kinda have to after last year—Dad’s hours got cut, we were 2 months behind on rent at the 32nd Street place (like $2,100), and I worked a ton of late 3:30–11 shifts at Desert Peaks. Ended up at Phoenix Baptist on 4/19/24 almost passing out.

Marco: That’s too much man. Let us know if you need rides or whatever.

Ethan: Appreciate it. Just need sleep and like 3 burritos.

Hi HR Team,

My name is Ethan Ramirez, and I’m a part-time sales associate at the Desert Peaks Sporting Goods location on McDowell. I wanted to send an update related to my workers’ compensation claim for the right ankle injury earlier this year.

On February 3, 2024, I sustained a right ankle inversion sprain while moving a 60-pound box in the stockroom. Since then I’ve been treated through Phoenix Sports Rehab in Phoenix. Physical therapist Mark Liu fitted me with a custom right ankle brace in February 2024. I’ve been wearing it consistently for my shifts, especially when I’m in the back lifting or restocking, and also for soccer games.

So far the brace has helped a lot with stability and I haven’t had any recurrent sprains. I’m still doing the strengthening exercises he gave me—calf raises, band work, balance drills, etc. I just wanted to confirm that continued use of the brace is covered under the original workers’ comp claim, and ask whether there is any paperwork needed if it needs to be replaced in the next year due to wear.

Also, while this isn’t directly about the injury, I’m trying to avoid late 3:30–11:00 pm closing shifts on school nights. In April I had a near-syncope episode and a 14-pound weight loss that landed me in the ER at Phoenix Baptist Hospital on April 19, 2024. My doctor recommended limiting those late shifts during the school year so I can keep up with sports and stay healthy.

Please let me know if you need any additional documentation from Phoenix Sports Rehab or my physician.

Thank you,
Ethan Ramirez

metadata.subject: Follow-up on workers’ comp ankle injury and brace

artifacts.content:
From: Ethan Ramirez <ethan.ramirez@email.com>
To: Mom <laura.ramirez@example.com>
Date: March 18, 2024
Subject: Therapy + soccer schedule stuff

Hey Mom,

I wanted to write this out so it’s all in one place instead of just catching you when you’re grading at the table.

After what happened at that club soccer tournament in Tucson, AZ a couple weekends ago (the panic episode on the sideline before the second game), Dr. Vanessa Ortiz at Valley Hope Behavioral Health Clinic, Phoenix, AZ thinks it would be smart for me to keep doing weekly telehealth counseling sessions. We started in March 2024 and she put in her notes that the recommendation is to continue the Zoom sessions through the 2021–22 school year sports season so I stay on top of performance-related anxiety and the sleep issues that pop up before big games.

I’m okay with it and honestly it’s been helping. The problem is just figuring out how to fit everything once Desert Vista practices ramp up again and I’m juggling work at Desert Peaks Sporting Goods and helping with Phoenix United Youth Soccer Club.

Can you help me look at the practice calendar and my shifts so we can email Valley Hope and lock in a regular time with Dr. Ortiz? She suggested Thursday evenings, but we might have to move it if there’s a conflict with away games.

Also, I know we don’t love revisiting this, but I keep thinking about Mountain View Family Medicine on 08/10/2021 when you asked them to run that urine screen and it came back positive for low-level THC from when I tried marijuana twice at a friend’s house near Papago Park in late July 2021. I really don’t want to break your trust like that again, so I’m trying to take this anxiety stuff seriously.

Love you,
Ethan

Personal note – saved in Google Keep
Title: Don’t repeat old mistakes
Date: 2024-04-02

I keep coming back to that sports physical at Mountain View Family Medicine, Phoenix, AZ on 08/10/2021. Mom (Laura Ramirez) was worried about peer vaping and asked for the urine test, and it ended up showing a urine screen positive for low-level THC. All from that brief experimentation with marijuana – literally just twice at a friend’s house near Papago Park in late July 2021. I told Dr. Patel then (and it’s still true) that I haven’t used anything since.

I need to remember how that felt: sitting there in the exam room, thinking I’d blown my whole season and Mom’s trust. I got lucky that there was no evidence of current substance misuse and that the doctor focused on education instead of punishment. But I don’t get a do-over if I mess up again.

Now with everything going on – finishing senior year at Desert Vista, shifts at Desert Peaks Sporting Goods, coaching with Phoenix United Youth Soccer Club, and trying to walk on at ASU – I can’t afford distractions. Between that old THC mess and the panic episode during the club soccer tournament in Tucson that led to starting weekly telehealth counseling sessions with Dr. Vanessa Ortiz at Valley Hope Behavioral Health Clinic in March 2024, I’ve had enough wake-up calls.

Bottom line: stay clean, keep going to therapy, protect my season, my scholarship chances, and my family’s trust.
